sourceMovie.title: Horrible Bosses
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Horrible Bosses works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Weekend at Bernie's
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Horrible Bosses clicked for you, Weekend at Bernie's (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Horrible Bosses?
faq.answer: Try Weekend at Bernie's, God Bless America, Bernie—each shares DNA with Horrible Bosses while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Horrible Bosses?
faq.answer: Horrible Bosses (2011) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Horrible Bosses part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Horrible Bosses Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
